data.title: Microservices
data.description: Spotify's backend is built on a microservices architecture, allowing different services to be developed and deployed independently.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes RESTful APIs, containers, and orchestration tools like Kubernetes.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Spotify uses distributed systems to manage its large user base and vast library of songs.
data.technical_spec: Employs technologies like Cassandra, Kafka, and AWS for scalability.

data.title: Data Processing Pipelines
data.description: Comprehensive data processing pipelines are in place to manage user data and personalize experiences.
data.technical_spec: Apache Beam along with Google Cloud Dataflow for batch and stream processing.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global network of servers improves load times and streaming quality for users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes edge caching and regional optimizations through partnerships with CDN providers.

data.title: Cross-Platform Compatibility
data.description: Spotify maintains a seamless experience across desktop, mobile, and web applications.
data.technical_spec: Uses frontend frameworks like React Native, and backend protocols that support both iOS and Android platforms.
